## 2026-07-12, Library Version 2026.07.317, PR #829

DORA reporting-window verification and citation (deep-assessment r2 F5; closes TODO §3.48). Library `2026.07.316` to `2026.07.317`.

### Changed

- [`compliance/financial-services/annex-dora-implementation.md`](../../compliance/financial-services/annex-dora-implementation.md) (`0.0.6` to `0.0.7`, Date to 2026-07-12): the pillar-2 reporting-windows sentence now states the initial-notification window with BOTH statutory bounds (within 4 hours of classification as a major incident AND no later than 24 hours from the moment the entity became aware of the incident), where it previously gave only the 4-hours-from-classification bound; the intermediate report is anchored to "at the latest within 72 hours of the initial notification" and the final report to "no later than one month after the intermediate report". Precise citations added: Delegated Reg (EU) 2024/1772 (classification and materiality thresholds), 2025/301 (report content and time limits), Implementing Reg (EU) 2025/302 (report forms), plus DORA Articles 18/19/20. The framework-reference table's "DORA RTS and ITS" row now names the incident-reporting instrument set.
- Regenerated [`taxonomy.yml`](../../taxonomy.yml), [`docs/portal.md`](../../docs/portal.md), [`docs/maturity-scorecard.md`](../../docs/maturity-scorecard.md) after the per-document Version bump.

### Verification

- Every window, the three DORA article numbers (18 classification / 19 reporting / 20 RTS-ITS mandate), and the instrument-to-function mapping were verified VERBATIM against the held reference texts in `grc_library_ref` (the held Delegated Reg (EU) 2025/301 incident-reporting extract for the windows, and the held base DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 extract for the article headings). A refute-briefed skeptical verifier re-checked the change against those held texts before push.
- The three instruments were ALREADY held in `grc_library_ref` (the full DORA second-level suite). A redundant ref-ingest branch (`claude/ingest-dora-rts-its`, ref PR #60) was opened after an initial grep of a stale local ref checkout (5 commits behind `origin/main`) misread them as absent; on resetting to `origin/main` and finding them held, the branch and PR were closed with no reference-side change. Lesson logged to the improvement log: fetch the sibling repo's `origin/main` before asserting its holdings.
- `tools/run_all_audits.sh` = 69/69 post-edit; the pre-push guard green.

### Fixed

- The DORA annex's under-stated initial-notification reporting window (the deep-assessment r2 F5 finding): a financial entity reading the prior text would see only the 4-hours-from-classification deadline and miss the 24-hours-from-awareness outer bound that the RTS also imposes.

### Discipline observation

- The `/validate-pr` #828 row and the `/retro` #828 row are batched into this PR (recursion-avoidance). The redundant-ingest episode is the maintainer's "check the ingest directory / the live reference state before downloading" directive validating itself: the miss was reading a stale LOCAL checkout, which the directive (and the reset to `origin/main`) caught before any duplicate landed on reference `main`.

All Dates co-bumped to 2026-07-12.

Whole-repo grep confirms 0 residual documentation-labeled 42001 §8.4 carriers.

### Added

- [`tools/lint-positional-backlog-tokens.py`](../../tools/lint-positional-backlog-tokens.py): gate 69, a corpus lint flagging renumber-fragile positional backlog references (a `TODO` / `backlog item` qualifier followed by a `§`- or `P`-prefixed or dotted section token) that should use a stable coded id or topic name. - Batched the #823 QA rows (recursion-avoidance): the #823 `/validate-pr` row (1 in-window note) in [`.working/validate-pr/history.md`](../validate-pr/history.md) (`1.2.594`->`1.2.595`) and the #823 `/retro` row in [`.working/improvement-log.md`](../improvement-log.md) (`1.0.533`->`1.0.534`).

### Verification

- **Skeptical verifier: SHIP** (after the gate-50 batch fix). The refute-briefed verifier confirmed the gate's substance correct and complete: the detection logic genuinely matches the target class (non-stub), the five-case fixture exercises both detect and clean paths, all three carrier rewords are meaning-preserving with 0 residual carriers corpus-wide and no false positives, four-surface parity + count at 69, and no stale "68"/"sixty-eight" in any scanned surface. It raised one error (the #823 QA rows unbatched, now fixed in this PR's diff) and one note (the latent `TODO item N.M` regex gap, routed to §3.50).
- **Landing method:** the held branch `positional-token-gate-69` (merge-base #820, three PRs behind main) was NOT git-rebased (its version/CHANGELOG/generated surfaces would all conflict and be re-authored anyway); instead the substance files (the gate, its four-surface wiring, the fixture, the spec gate-69 rows, the carrier rewords, the SKILL count) were taken onto a fresh branch off current `main` via `git checkout <branch> -- <files>`, confirmed safe because 0 commits touched any of those files on `main` since #820, and the version/CHANGELOG/DONE bookkeeping was authored fresh for #824.
- **Gate re-derivation:** gate 69 was run on the current `main` state and returned 0 residual carriers ("no renumber-fragile positional backlog-token references in corpus prose"), confirming the carrier set is stable since the branch's derivation and that #821-#823 introduced no new positional tokens.
- Full suite green (69 of 69 gates) via the pre-push guard; gate 35 four-surface parity at 69, gate 39 count consistency at 69, gate 64 detailed-prose presence for gate 69, and the gate-69 regression fixture (gate 36) all pass. The change was independently checked by a refute-briefed skeptical verifier before push.
- A note for the maintainer: gate 60 (guardrail cadence) now shows drift 1 (68 to 69), below its threshold of 3, because the r8 `/guardrails` pass of record (#823) reset the cadence at 68 gates the turn before this addition.

## 2026-07-11, Library Version 2026.07.305, PR #817

Register-currency drift-check tool (closes TODO §1.7), the currency-ledger plan's advisory sync tool. Library `2026.07.304` to `2026.07.305`.

### Added

- [`tools/audit-register-currency.py`](../../tools/audit-register-currency.py): advisory cross-repo drift report between the `grc_library_ref` currency ledger and [`governance/register-canonical-citations.md`](../../governance/register-canonical-citations.md). Five advisory checks (register-behind, version-disagreement, upstream-URL-mismatch, ledger-held-stale, unmatched-rows); exact-then-shortest-prefix designation matching (the register Standard-ID stem is matched as a word-boundary prefix of a ledger title, and among several prefix matches the shortest base-standard title wins so a verbose draft or overlay does not capture the match); edition-token version comparison (a year or a revision number, so a bare year versus a year-with-parenthetical does not false-flag); a stdlib-only line parser for the generated two-space-indent catalogue (no third-party YAML); a `--ledger` option (env `GRC_REF_PATH` fallback, then the sibling `grc_library_ref` checkout), a `--register` option, a `--strict` mode (exit 2 on the two substantive checks, never CI-wired), and a `--self-test`. Advisory pattern per [`tools/audit-brief-freshness.py`](../../tools/audit-brief-freshness.py): named `audit-*` (not gate-discovered), not wired into the runner, the CI workflow, or pre-commit, exits 0 by default, and skips with exit 0 when the reference checkout is absent.

### Changed

- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md): §1.7 removed and rotated to [`.working/DONE.md`](../DONE.md).
- [`.working/validate-pr/history.md`](../validate-pr/history.md) (`1.2.587`->`1.2.588`) and [`.working/improvement-log.md`](../improvement-log.md) (`1.0.527`->`1.0.528`): the batched #816 `/validate-pr` (clean) and `/retro` rows.

### Verification

- `--self-test` passes (7 inline unittests: normalization, the ledger and register parsers, the five-check report, the edition-token comparison including the same-year no-flag case, and the shortest-prefix matcher). A live run against the held ledger matched 43 register rows (92 unmatched-informational, expected for cited-but-not-held sources) and surfaced 4 register-behind rows, 1 version-disagreement (WCO SAFE, 2021 held versus 2025 register), and 1 held-stale (WCO SAFE), all plausible and useful. All 68 audit gates still pass (the advisory tool is not gate-wired).
- Substantive tier (a new tool with parsing and matching logic): one refute-briefed skeptical verifier pre-push. It caught a matcher defect fixed in-window: the flagship "OWASP Top 10" mis-matched the "Proactive Controls" sibling because a mid-string edition year ("OWASP Top 10:2025 (Web Application Security Risks)") escaped the end-anchored year-strip, yielding a spurious version-disagreement. Fixed by stripping a trailing parenthetical before the edition year in `normalize_designation` (with a new self-test fixture), which restored the exact flagship match and removed the false finding; the corrected tally is stated above.

### Note

- The spec's optional register-seeding step (refresh the register rows the tool flags as behind, from the ledger) is NOT done in this PR; it is a separate register-data change that overlaps TODO §1.8 (WCO SAFE ref-side) and benefits from the tool now existing to target it. Wiring the tool into the `/resume` step-3 advisory cadence (alongside [`tools/audit-brief-freshness.py`](../../tools/audit-brief-freshness.py) and [`tools/audit-delivery-status.py`](../../tools/audit-delivery-status.py)) is a small follow-up protected [`.claude/CLAUDE.md`](../../.claude/CLAUDE.md) edit.

## 2026-07-11, Library Version 2026.07.303, PR #815

AI gaps-and-expansion workstream PR 9 (A5), the value-and-decision-governance cluster; completes the workstream's content builds (PR 1 through 10). Library `2026.07.302` to `2026.07.303`.

### Added

- [`ai/framework-ai-value-and-decision-governance.md`](../../ai/framework-ai-value-and-decision-governance.md) (`0.0.1`): new Framework. Sections: Purpose (value and decision quality as governance evidence, distinct from the accountability/risk framework and the process-maturity framework); Scope (with explicit out-of-scope hand-offs to A4, framework-ai-governance-and-risk, the cost-governance standard, and the model-lifecycle procedure); the three-tier value-maturity ladder (Production / Scaled / Transformation) as a governance signal, tabled with the evidence a reviewer inspects and explicitly NOT the CMMI capability ladder; the idea-stage AI use-case decision record (fixed KPIs, named accountable owner, anticipated audit requirements, each cross-referencing the existing corpus construct rather than redefining it); workflow embedding and per-deployment-surface adoption tracking as post-deployment governance evidence (a genuinely new construct A5 owns); the green-vs-brown-dollars benefits taxonomy (net-new value versus cost avoidance, explicitly financial not environmental); Framework alignment; Limitations.

### Changed

- [`ai/README.md`](../../ai/README.md) (`1.1.12`->`1.1.13`) and [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md) (`1.27.81`->`1.27.82`): listing rows for the new Framework.
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md) §2.2: updated to mark all PR 1-10 content builds COMPLETE (A5 = PR 9; A6c = PR 10 / #809), with only the maintainer-invoked fresh-session closing `/deep-assessment` remaining; §2.2 stays open for that step only.
- In-window fix for the #814 `/validate-pr` LOW finding: the #814 records misnamed the repointed sweep-model citation's section as §3.14 (ETSI); the actual repoint was in §3.36 (improvement-log cycle-to-scratch). Corrected `§3.14`->`§3.36` in [`CHANGELOG.md`](../../CHANGELOG.md), this detailed mirror, [`.working/DONE.md`](../DONE.md), and [`.working/validate-pr/history.md`](../validate-pr/history.md).
- [`.working/validate-pr/history.md`](../validate-pr/history.md) (`1.2.585`->`1.2.586`) and [`.working/improvement-log.md`](../improvement-log.md) (`1.0.525`->`1.0.526`): the batched #814 `/validate-pr` and `/retro` rows.

### Verification

- Held-source grounding (research subagent + orchestrator re-read): the CMU SEI AI Adoption Maturity Model v1.0 is HELD (`grc_library_ref/frameworks/SEI/`), copyright-restricted with partial-re-use-with-attribution authorized (2026-07-04); its five-level progression (Exploratory / Implemented / Aligned / Scaled / Future Ready) corroborates the three-tier value ladder as a governance signal. It is cited by reference only, not reproduced, reusing A4's exact reference-only disclaimer phrasing.
- The "Pilot to Production" playbook (the value-delivery seed) is NOT held; A5 is authored as original governance constructs, with the playbook cited by reference as origin only and no content reproduced. This resolves the pending A5 decision toward authoring-as-original (the maintainer's complete-2.2 direction).
- Corpus-fit: A5 is a new standalone Framework (zero content overlap with framework-ai-governance-and-risk; distinct value/decision axis from A4's process-maturity axis); the green-vs-brown taxonomy has zero corpus collision (no environmental-"green" content), and a one-line financial-not-environmental definition is included; the accountable-owner, KPI, audit, and cost constructs are cross-referenced, not duplicated.
- All new/changed docs Version+Date co-bumped (A5 0.0.1; ai/README 1.1.13; register 1.27.82; all 2026-07-11); taxonomy/portal/scorecard regenerated (A5 present). All 68 audit gates pass.
- Substantive tier (new citable Framework): one refute-briefed skeptical verifier pre-push.

### Discipline observation

- A sequencing error was caught and recovered mid-work: #814 (the prior PR) was pushed but not opened/merged before the A5 branch was cut from main, stranding #814's changes on its branch. Recovery: committed the A5 WIP, opened and merged #814, then rebased the A5 branch onto the #814-inclusive main (clean, disjoint files). Recorded so the pre-branch-cut merge-confirmation step is reinforced.

- [`privacy/procedure-privacy-impact-and-cross-border-transfer.md`](../../privacy/procedure-privacy-impact-and-cross-border-transfer.md) (`1.6.0`->`1.6.1`): the framework-alignment row "AI Impact Assessment Obligations (Canada) | AIDA section 29" becomes "AI and automated-decision impact obligations (Canada)" mapped to the Treasury Board Directive on Automated Decision-Making section 6.1 (Algorithmic Impact Assessment; federal institutions) and Quebec Law 25 sections 3.3 and 12.1 (private sector).
- [`privacy/annex-privacy-jurisdiction-index.md`](../../privacy/annex-privacy-jurisdiction-index.md) (`1.0.12`->`1.0.13`): the Canada nav row and the Canada detail row drop the "CPPA (pending)/if enacted" and "AIDA (pending)" language (both lapsed with Bill C-27, January 2025); the AI-specific-obligation cell now names Quebec Law 25 section 12.1 automated-decision review and the federal TBS Directive Algorithmic Impact Assessment, with the AIDA high-impact regime marked lapsed.
- [`governance/matrix-cross-framework-alignment.md`](../../governance/matrix-cross-framework-alignment.md) (`1.1.11`->`1.1.12`): the AI-model-governance row's "AIDA (pending, Canada)" becomes "Treasury Board Directive on Automated Decision-Making (Canada; AIDA lapsed)".
- [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md) (`1.27.80`->`1.27.81`): the privacy-Canada annex key-reference cell's "AIDA (pending)" (co-located with "CPPA (pending)") normalized to "Quebec Law 25 (CPPA/AIDA lapsed)"; the impact-assessment procedure's key-reference cell "AIDA §29" updated to "Quebec Law 25 §3.3/§12.1" to match the procedure body (paired-surface fix).
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md): §1.10 removed (resolved) and rotated to [`.working/DONE.md`](../DONE.md); §3.45 added (the optional neutral-AIDA-bare-listing annotation the #810 skeptical verifier surfaced, explicitly optional).
- [`.working/pending-decisions.md`](../pending-decisions.md): the §1.10 replacement-mapping stricter-safe default recorded for confirm-or-redirect, with two upstream date-verification items surfaced.
- [`.working/validate-pr/history.md`](../validate-pr/history.md) (`1.2.580`->`1.2.581`) and [`.working/improvement-log.md`](../improvement-log.md) (`1.0.520`->`1.0.521`): the batched #809 `/validate-pr` (clean) and `/retro` rows.

### Verification

- Held-source grounding (research subagent + orchestrator re-read): the Treasury Board Directive on Automated Decision-Making (held; section 6.1 Algorithmic Impact Assessment obligation; scope section 5.1/8.1 = federal institutions, NOT private sector) and Quebec Law 25 (held official consolidation to 2025-12-11; section 3.3 PIA for "any person carrying on an enterprise", section 12.1 automated-decision notice). AIDA section 29's text is NOT held, so its content was not reconstructed.
- Scope match confirmed: Quebec Law 25 binds private-sector organizations (the DPIA template's audience); the TBS Directive binds federal institutions only and is cited as a clearly-labelled analogue, avoiding a scope mismatch.
- Corpus-wide bare-token `AIDA` grep after the edits: no surviving live-framing carrier; the four neutral bare-listing cells (in the governance exception-and-risk-acceptance policy, the security data-classification standard, and two reference-list cells of the governance document-index register) correctly left neutral; the paired procedure key-reference cell in the document-index register (line 131) caught and fixed.
- All five bodies-changed docs Version+Date co-bumped (2026-07-11); taxonomy regenerated first, then portal/scorecard.
- Substantive tier: one refute-briefed skeptical verifier pre-push.

### Discipline observation

- The corpus-wide `AIDA` grep surfaced one carrier the initial 5-carrier enumeration missed: the governance document-index register's line-131 cell (the impact-assessment procedure's key-reference) still said "AIDA section 29" after the procedure body was changed, a paired-surface incompleteness. Caught by the closing grep §1.10 itself mandated, fixed in-window before commit. Confirms the value of the bare-token corpus-wide grep over an enumerated input-set.

## 2026-07-11, Library Version 2026.07.297, PR #809

AI gaps-and-expansion workstream PR 10 (A6c), synthetic-content provenance: a new Guideline for the technical "how" of AI-generated-content transparency, anchored to the held NIST AI 100-4 and EU AI Act Article 50. Batches the #808 `/validate-pr` (clean) and `/retro` rows. Library `2026.07.296` to `2026.07.297`.

### Added

- [`ai/guideline-ai-synthetic-content-provenance.md`](../../ai/guideline-ai-synthetic-content-provenance.md) (`0.0.1`): new Guideline. Sections: Purpose (implementation guidance beneath policy Section 9.2's what/when); Scope (AI-generated output; excludes synthetic-training-input labelling and model-supply-chain provenance, both cross-referenced); the digital content transparency techniques (provenance data tracking, watermarking overt/covert, metadata recording; the three detection categories) attributed to NIST AI 100-4 as an informative overview; technique-selection attributes and limitations (detectability, robustness/stripping, generator-specific detectors, the covert-watermark privacy risk); disclosure and labelling (indirect versus direct); standards and specifications (C2PA and others cited only as NIST AI 100-4 reports them, primary texts not held); testing and evaluation; regulatory anchoring (EU AI Act Article 50(2)/(4)/(5)/(7), Regulation (EU) 2024/1689, with the pending Digital Omnibus deferred to the EU annex); Framework alignment; Limitations (techniques immature, honest-backstop framing).

### Changed

- [`ai/policy-ai-compliance.md`](../../ai/policy-ai-compliance.md) (`1.0.9`->`1.0.10`): Section 9.2 gains a cross-reference to the new guideline for the technically-implemented labelling mechanism.
- [`ai/README.md`](../../ai/README.md) (`1.1.11`->`1.1.12`) and [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md) (`1.27.79`->`1.27.80`): guideline listing rows added.
- [`.working/pending-decisions.md`](../pending-decisions.md): the A5 (PR 9) deferral recorded (playbook seed not held); the privacy-Canada "anticipated AIDA" pending entry marked resolved (in #805).
- [`.working/validate-pr/history.md`](../validate-pr/history.md) (`1.2.579`->`1.2.580`) and [`.working/improvement-log.md`](../improvement-log.md) (`1.0.519`->`1.0.520`): the batched #808 `/validate-pr` (clean) and `/retro` rows.

### Verification

- Every NIST AI 100-4 technique description and the EU AI Act Article 50 provisions were re-verified verbatim against the held sources (a research subagent extracted the locators; the orchestrator re-opened the key passages). NIST AI 100-4 is attributed as an informative overview ("describes / surveys"), never as a mandate, per the claim-fit discipline; the C2PA and other specifications are cited only as NIST AI 100-4 reports them (their primary texts are not held).
- Currency: NIST AI 100-4 (November 2024 final) confirmed current upstream this turn; EU AI Act Article 50 is the in-force Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 text, with the not-adopted Digital Omnibus amendment deferred to the EU jurisdiction annex.
- Substantive tier (a new citable document): one refute-briefed skeptical verifier pre-push. Guideline Version 0.0.1; all touched surfaces Version+Date co-bumped; taxonomy/portal/scorecard regenerated after the version bumps.

### Discipline observation

- PR 9 (A5) was deferred rather than built: its value-ladder and green-versus-brown-dollars taxonomies derive from the unheld Canada AI Alliance "Pilot to Production" playbook, and authoring a new citable framework's specific structure from a plan-summary of an unheld seed would risk accuracy and attribution. This is an accuracy-driven scoping decision (recorded in pending-decisions), not a degradation stop; the well-sourced A6c was built instead per the maintainer's "be most productive" direction.

## 2026-07-11, Library Version 2026.07.296, PR #808

AI gaps-and-expansion workstream, nested-markdown-link detection gate 68 (TODO 3.44, part 2 of 2, closes it) plus the #807 `/validate-pr` Finding-1 fix. Batches the #807 `/validate-pr` and `/retro` rows. Library `2026.07.295` to `2026.07.296`.

### Added

- [`tools/lint-nested-markdown-links.py`](../../tools/lint-nested-markdown-links.py): gate 68, a nested-markdown-link malformation audit. Detects the nested-link shape (a link whose visible text is itself a link, the doubly-wrapped form), which renders as a broken literal open bracket plus a dangling inline-link fragment and is gate-blind to the link-coverage gate (the inner link is well-formed) and the broken-link gate (the inner link resolves). Code-span-aware: it masks each inline code span to a placeholder before applying the regex, so a backticked description of the pattern is not flagged (the #807 Finding-1 constraint) while a real malformation's live brackets survive. Fence-aware via the shared iterator; scans the default markdown population (the root changelog included; `.working` and `.claude` exempt). Build-time census: zero, so the gate is preventive.
- [`tests/test_linters.py`](../../tests/test_linters.py): a `NestedMarkdownLinkTests` regression class (detect a real nested link; skip a backticked code-span description; skip a plain link and a `[[wikilink]]`).

### Changed

- Four-surface wiring for gate 68: [`.github/workflows/quality.yml`](../../.github/workflows/quality.yml), [`tools/run_all_audits.sh`](../../tools/run_all_audits.sh), [`.pre-commit-config.yaml`](../../.pre-commit-config.yaml), and [`governance/specification-audit-programme.md`](../../governance/specification-audit-programme.md) (`1.16.64`->`1.16.65`; the section 6 inventory row, the section 5 "Programme and index integrity" grouped list, and the per-gate detailed-prose paragraph).
- [`.working/changelog-details/CHANGELOG-detailed.md`](CHANGELOG-detailed.md): the #807 detailed entry's verification prose (Finding 1) tightened from the overstated "grep returns zero `[[`" to an accurate statement that no malformed nested links remain (the only `[[` tokens are that entry's own code-span descriptions of the pattern).
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md) and [`.working/DONE.md`](../DONE.md): TODO 3.44 deleted and rotated to DONE (both parts complete).
- [`.working/validate-pr/history.md`](../validate-pr/history.md) (`1.2.578`->`1.2.579`) and [`.working/improvement-log.md`](../improvement-log.md) (`1.0.518`->`1.0.519`): the batched #807 `/validate-pr` (Finding 1 fixed here) and `/retro` rows.

### Verification

- The gate was unit-tested at build (detect a real nested link; skip a backticked description, a plain link, and a wiki-style double bracket) and run corpus-wide (419 files, zero findings, so it lands green and preventive). Gate 35 (audit-programme parity) confirms the four surfaces agree; gate 39 counts the section 6 rows (now 68); gate 64 confirms the per-gate detailed-prose is present; the regression suite (gate 36) exercises the new test class.
- Substantive tier (a new gate): one refute-briefed skeptical verifier pre-push, plus guard-first (the gate is green on the post-#807 corpus). Pre-push guard green.

## 2026-07-11, Library Version 2026.07.291, PR #803

AI gaps-and-expansion workstream PR 8 (incident response and cost): deepens two AI operational documents against held sources (NIST AI 600-1, NIST AI 100-2e2025, OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications v2.0, ISO/IEC TR 27563:2023). - [`ai/plan-ai-incident-response.md`](../../ai/plan-ai-incident-response.md) (`1.0.6`->`1.0.7`): added a NIST AI 100-2e2025 taxonomy crosswalk under the incident-classes table (classes mapped to NISTAML.01 to NISTAML.05, with the specific subclass IDs for prompt injection, data poisoning, and the privacy-compromise family); added an "AI incident reporting and disclosure channels" section anchored to NIST AI 600-1 GV-2.1-001 (the AI Incident Database, AVID, CVE, NVD, OECD AI incident monitor; external disclosure framed as an AI Governance Council decision, not an operator default); deepened the framework-alignment table by replacing the generic NIST AI RMF row with a NIST AI 600-1 (Generative AI Profile) row citing GV-1.5-002, GV-2.1-001, MG-2.3-001, MG-4.3-001, and MG-4.3-003, and adding a NIST AI 100-2e2025 row and an ISO/IEC TR 27563:2023 row (informative supporting material for privacy-implicated paths, not a normative incident source).

- [`.working/validate-pr/history.md`](../validate-pr/history.md) (`1.2.573`->`1.2.574`) and [`.working/improvement-log.md`](../improvement-log.md) (`1.0.513`->`1.0.514`): the batched #802 `/validate-pr` history row (clean) and `/retro` row.
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md): added item 3.44 (CHANGELOG nested-link malformation: historical cleanup of the two pre-existing project-instruction-file nested links plus a candidate detection gate), from the #802 retrospective.
- [`.working/pending-decisions.md`](../pending-decisions.md): a 2026-07-11 daytime-round resolved block recording the maintainer's mode-switch answers (continue the workstream; correct issues then wind down and run `/deep-assessment` in a fresh session; protected-file backlog authorized after PR 10; the A4 5-tier ladder and A3=Guide/A4=Framework defaults confirmed; address more issues before wind-down absent degradation; confirm with the maintainer at the wind-down point).

### Verification

- Every NIST AI 600-1 action ID (GV-1.5-002, GV-2.1-001, MG-2.3-001, MG-4.3-001, MG-4.3-003), the NIST AI 100-2e2025 NISTAML taxonomy IDs, the OWASP LLM10:2025 text (Unbounded Consumption / Denial of Wallet), and the NIST AI 600-1 section 2.5 text were re-verified verbatim against the held `grc_library_ref` sources at apply time (a research subagent extracted them; the orchestrator re-opened each locator). The held source's wording "OECD AI incident monitor" (lower case) was used, not the prompt's variant.
- Currency: the load-bearing new anchors (NIST AI 600-1, NIST AI 100-2e2025, OWASP LLM Top 10 v2.0) were confirmed current upstream earlier this session; ISO/IEC TR 27563:2023 is cited as informative supporting material only.
- Substantive-tier change: one refute-briefed skeptical verifier pre-push. Pre-push guard green (67/67 corpus gates + PR-time delta gates). Both documents' Version and Date co-bumped; taxonomy and scorecard regenerated.

## 2026-07-11, Library Version 2026.07.290, PR #802

AI gaps-and-expansion workstream, canonical-register enrollment for the AI jurisdiction annexes: addresses the #800 `/validate-pr` finding (Canada annex's currency-sensitive AI instruments absent from the canonical-citations register) and extends the enrollment to the Australia annex's instruments. Batches the #801 `/validate-pr` (clean) and `/retro` rows. Library `2026.07.289` to `2026.07.290`.

### Added

- [`governance/register-canonical-citations.md`](../../governance/register-canonical-citations.md) (`1.5.20`->`1.5.21`): four Canada AI instruments added to the North-American section, a new Asia-Pacific regulations and frameworks section added with four Australia voluntary AI instruments, and the existing Australia Privacy Act row refreshed for the automated-decision limb:
  - **Canada (North-American section):** Treasury Board Directive on Automated Decision-Making (in force since 2019; 24 June 2025 to 24 June 2026 transition for the third-review amendments); OSFI Guideline E-23 (final 11 September 2025, effective 1 May 2027); Ontario EDSTA (Royal Assent 25 November 2024, AI provisions not yet proclaimed); CAN/DGSI 101:2025 (2nd edition).
  - **Australia (new Asia-Pacific regulations and frameworks section):** National AI Plan 2025 (launched 2 December 2025); AI Ethics Principles (published 7 November 2019, updated 2 December 2025); Voluntary AI Safety Standard (National AI Centre, August 2024); Guidance for AI Adoption (National AI Centre, 21 October 2025).

### Changed

- [`governance/register-canonical-citations.md`](../../governance/register-canonical-citations.md): the existing Australia Privacy Act row refreshed from "1988 (with 2024 amendments)" to record the Privacy and Other Legislation Amendment Act 2024 (Cth) No. 128, 2024 (Royal Assent 10 December 2024) and the automated-decision transparency obligation (APP 1.7 to 1.9, Schedule 1 Part 15) commencing 10 December 2026; Last verified refreshed to 2026-07-11.
- [`tools/lint-external-link-domains.py`](../../tools/lint-external-link-domains.py) and [`governance/specification-citation-verification.md`](../../governance/specification-citation-verification.md) (`1.2.13`->`1.2.14`): five authoritative publisher domains added to the external-link-domain allow-list (both the linter `ALLOW_LIST` and the section 7.1 publisher table, paired) for the newly-enrolled register URLs: `osfi-bsif.gc.ca`, `ola.org`, `dgc-cgn.org` (Canada), and `industry.gov.au`, `ai.gov.au` (Australia); `tbs-sct.canada.ca` is already suffix-covered by `canada.ca`. A section 7.2 additions-log entry records the change.
- [`.working/validate-pr/history.md`](../validate-pr/history.md) (`1.2.572`->`1.2.573`) and [`.working/improvement-log.md`](../improvement-log.md) (`1.0.512`->`1.0.513`): the batched #801 `/validate-pr` history row (clean, zero-finding, no per-PR record file) and `/retro` row.

### Verification

- Every enrolled instrument's version, publication date, and upstream check URL was confirmed upstream this turn (the TBS Directive at tbs-sct.canada.ca; OSFI E-23 final 2025-09-11 effective 2027-05-01; Ontario Bill 194 EDSTA AI provisions unproclaimed as of mid-2026; the DISR/NAIC Australia instruments at industry.gov.au / ai.gov.au; the Privacy Act amending Act C2024A00128 with the Part 15 commencement table). No URL was written without upstream confirmation this turn.
- [`tools/lint-standards-currency.py`](../../tools/lint-standards-currency.py) match logic confirmed safe for the new "superseded" markers (`1st edition` for CAN/DGSI, `Draft 2023-11-20` for OSFI E-23): the linter matches only a Standard ID immediately followed by the superseded marker, so no corpus-wide false positive.
- Substantive-tier change: one refute-briefed skeptical verifier pre-push. Pre-push guard green (67/67 corpus gates + PR-time delta gates).

### Discipline observation

- The register taxonomy placed the Canada regulatory instruments in the North-American section (parallel to the Colorado AI Act and NYC LL144 rows) and created a new Asia-Pacific section for the Australia voluntary AI instruments (parallel to the EU and North-American regional sections); the Australia Privacy Act binding limb stayed in its existing "Other privacy regulations" row (refreshed) rather than duplicated. This enrollment was split into its own PR (not crammed into the sensitive #800/#801 annex PRs) per always-split-when-in-doubt, keeping those annexes' dual-verifier scope clean. The pre-push skeptical verifier caught a HIGH-severity defect fixed before push: a global replace of a bare code span (the currency linter's filename), meant to link it in the new entry, also re-wrapped that code span inside two pre-existing historical entries' links (PR #505 and PR #359), producing malformed nested links; the fix reverted those two historical lines to their well-formed single-link form, leaving only the new entry's links added. The lesson (use a scoped replacement, not a bare-token global one, when a token already appears inside links elsewhere in the file) is logged for the hallucination-metrics ledger in the next PR. Two further pre-existing malformed nested links in unrelated early-PR historical entries (project-instruction-file references) are left untouched as out-of-scope and routed as a separate cleanup.

## 2026-07-11, Library Version 2026.07.289, PR #801

AI gaps-and-expansion workstream PR 7 (jurisdictions), unit 3, the Australia AI annex: creates [`ai/jurisdictions/annex-ai-australia.md`](../../ai/jurisdictions/annex-ai-australia.md), a per-regime view of Australia's voluntary-and-principles-based AI governance, mirroring the Canada, Colorado, and EU annexes. Australia has no comprehensive AI statute: its regime is built on largely technology-neutral existing law, supplemented by the voluntary AI Ethics Principles (8 principles), the voluntary Voluntary AI Safety Standard (10 guardrails), and the current Guidance for AI Adoption (6 essential practices, condensing the 10 guardrails), with the National AI Plan 2025 (3 goals, establishing an AI Safety Institute) as strategy, and one binding AI-adjacent obligation: the Privacy Act 1988 automated-decision transparency requirement (new Australian Privacy Principles 1.7 to 1.9, inserted by the Privacy and Other Legislation Amendment Act 2024), a privacy-policy-content duty that commences 10 December 2026. Each instrument's status (voluntary, strategy, or binding) is stated accurately, and the proposed mandatory guardrails and the AI Safety Institute's statutory footing are framed as prospective. The annex is listed in [`ai/README.md`](../../ai/README.md) and the [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md). All Australia facts were verified against the held `grc_library_ref` trusted sources this turn, and the Privacy Act automated-decision commencement (10 December 2026, 24 months after Royal Assent on 10 December 2024) confirmed upstream. Batches the #800 `/validate-pr` and `/retro` rows. Library `2026.07.288` to `2026.07.289`.

### Added

- [`ai/jurisdictions/annex-ai-australia.md`](../../ai/jurisdictions/annex-ai-australia.md) (`0.0.1`): new Australia AI jurisdiction annex. Sections: Purpose; the voluntary-and-principles-based regulatory posture (no omnibus AI statute, largely technology-neutral existing law); the National AI Plan 2025 (3 goals; the AI Safety Institute being established); the AI Ethics Principles (8, voluntary; published 7 November 2019, updated 2 December 2025); the Voluntary AI Safety Standard (10 guardrails, voluntary, National AI Centre August 2024); the Guidance for AI Adoption (6 essential practices, voluntary, 21 October 2025, condensing the 10 guardrails); the one binding AI-adjacent obligation (Privacy Act 1988 APP 1.7 to 1.9 automated-decision transparency, a privacy-policy-content duty commencing 10 December 2026); the relationship to the Australian privacy regime (cross-reference to the privacy-Australia annex); adopter-role framing; Limitations (voluntary-versus-binding and not-yet-in-force statuses called out); Framework alignment (NIST AI RMF, ISO/IEC 42001).

### Changed

- [`ai/README.md`](../../ai/README.md) (`1.1.10`->`1.1.11`) and [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md) (`1.27.78`->`1.27.79`): Australia annex listing rows added.
- [`.working/validate-pr/history.md`](../validate-pr/history.md) (`1.2.571`->`1.2.572`) and [`.working/improvement-log.md`](../improvement-log.md) (`1.0.511`->`1.0.512`): the batched #800 `/validate-pr` history row and `/retro` row (the #800 per-PR record is [`.working/validate-pr/2026-07-11-PR-800.md`](../validate-pr/2026-07-11-PR-800.md)).

### Verification

- All facts verified against the held `grc_library_ref` trusted sources (framework-tier DISR/NAIC instruments and the legislation-tier Privacy and Other Legislation Amendment Act 2024): the 8 AI Ethics Principles verbatim; the 10 Voluntary AI Safety Standard guardrails verbatim; the National AI Plan 2025 three goals verbatim (capturing the opportunity; spreading the benefits; keeping Australians safe, with the AI Safety Institute being established and existing law "largely technology-neutral"); the Guidance for AI Adoption six essential practices verbatim (decide who is accountable; understand impacts and plan accordingly; measure and manage risks; share essential information; test and monitor; maintain human control); and the Privacy Act Part 15 automated-decision provisions (APP 1.7 to 1.9) with the commencement table confirming 10 December 2026.
- The Privacy Act automated-decision commencement (10 December 2026) was additionally confirmed upstream this turn (OAIC and legal-industry sources), consistent with the held Act's commencement table.
- Pre-push skeptical verification: sensitive jurisdiction annex, treated with the dual high-assurance harness (independent accuracy/false-positive lens + completeness/source-discipline/false-negative lens). Both verifiers independently converged on one precision warning, fixed pre-push: the APP 1.8 paraphrase read "made or substantially assisted", dropping the statute's "solely" distinction between limb (b) (decisions made **solely** by the program) and limb (c) (decisions **substantially assisted**), now corrected to state all three 1.8 limbs faithfully. Two further notes fixed pre-push: an "OAIC is expected to publish guidance" sentence that rested on no held source (dropped, to keep the annex fully held-source-grounded), and the AISI framing tightened to the held National AI Plan wording (a monitoring, testing, and information-sharing body supporting existing regulators, not itself a regulator). One note is not this annex's defect and is already routed: the cross-referenced [`privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-australia.md`](../../privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-australia.md) still carries a looser "disclosed when requested" ADM phrasing that this annex correctly rejects (privacy-annex reconciliation is a routed TODO).
- Acronyms expanded on first use (DISR, NAIC, OAIC, AISI, APP); no em/en dashes; no bare `ensure`. The `australia` filename token shares with the title (gate 7). All Version and Date surfaces bumped; taxonomy/portal/scorecard regenerated after the version bumps.

### Discipline observation

- The #800 `/validate-pr` Subagent A surfaced that the Canada annex's six currency-sensitive instruments were not enrolled in the canonical-citations register. The same enrollment applies to this Australia annex's instruments. Per always-split-when-in-doubt, the enrollment (Canada + Australia instruments, each with per-row upstream currency confirmation) is made the dedicated next PR rather than crammed into this sensitive annex PR, keeping the dual-verifier scope clean. The finding is tracked (in the #800 record and the improvement log), not dropped.

## 2026-07-11, Library Version 2026.07.288, PR #800

AI gaps-and-expansion workstream PR 7 (jurisdictions), unit 2, the Canada AI annex: creates [`ai/jurisdictions/annex-ai-canada.md`](../../ai/jurisdictions/annex-ai-canada.md), a per-regime view of Canada's patchwork AI governance, mirroring the Colorado and EU annexes. Canada has no in-force comprehensive AI statute (the Artificial Intelligence and Data Act (AIDA) lapsed when Bill C-27 died at prorogation and, per a June 2025 ministerial statement, will not return in its original form), so the annex presents the binding federal public-sector Treasury Board Directive on Automated Decision-Making and its Algorithmic Impact Assessment, the advisory FASTER generative-AI guide, the voluntary ISED Code of Conduct, the AI for All national strategy, the Ontario Enhancing Digital Security and Trust Act (enacted but not yet in force), OSFI Guideline E-23 (effective 1 May 2027), and CAN/DGSI 101:2025, each with its status stated accurately. Also carries the PR-1 (#791) fix: [`ai/procedure-ai-system-impact-assessment.md`](../../ai/procedure-ai-system-impact-assessment.md) Step 7 corrected the Treasury Board Directive characterization from "in force 24 June 2025" to "in force since 2019, with updated requirements phasing in on a 24 June 2025 to 24 June 2026 compliance transition" (2025-06-24 is a compliance-transition date, not the Directive's in-force date). The annex is listed in [`ai/README.md`](../../ai/README.md) and the [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md). The Canada regulatory-status facts were verified upstream this turn (AIDA lapsed at prorogation; Ontario EDSTA proclamation-pending; the Treasury Board Directive 2019 in-force with the 2025/2026 transition), and the held instruments' provisions were extracted by a research subagent. Batches the #799 `/validate-pr` and `/retro` rows. The Australia annex and the light EU/Colorado re-verify follow. Library `2026.07.287` to `2026.07.288`.

### Added

- [`ai/jurisdictions/annex-ai-canada.md`](../../ai/jurisdictions/annex-ai-canada.md) (`0.0.1`): new Canada AI jurisdiction annex. Sections: Purpose; the patchwork-not-a-statute landscape (AIDA lapsed); the Treasury Board Directive on Automated Decision-Making (in force since 2019, 2025/2026 transition); the Algorithmic Impact Assessment (65 risk + 41 mitigation questions, impact levels I-IV); the FASTER generative-AI guide; the ISED Voluntary Code (six outcomes, voluntary); AI for All (six pillars); Ontario EDSTA (enacted, not yet in force); OSFI Guideline E-23 (effective 2027-05-01); CAN/DGSI 101:2025 (voluntary); adopter-role framing; the privacy cross-reference; Limitations; Framework alignment.

### Changed

- [`ai/procedure-ai-system-impact-assessment.md`](../../ai/procedure-ai-system-impact-assessment.md) (`1.0.5`->`1.0.6`): PR-1 (#791) fix, the Treasury Board Directive characterization corrected from "in force 24 June 2025" to "in force since 2019, with updated requirements phasing in on a 24 June 2025 to 24 June 2026 compliance transition".
- [`ai/README.md`](../../ai/README.md) (`1.1.9`->`1.1.10`) and [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md) (`1.27.77`->`1.27.78`): Canada annex listing rows added.
- Generated artefacts ([`taxonomy.yml`](../../taxonomy.yml), [`docs/portal.md`](../../docs/portal.md), [`docs/maturity-scorecard.md`](../../docs/maturity-scorecard.md)) regenerated.

### Verification

- Canada regulatory-status facts verified upstream this turn: AIDA (Bill C-27 Part 3) died on the Order Paper at the 2025-01-06 prorogation and will not return in its original form (matches the canonical register's Canada AIDA lapsed row); the Ontario EDSTA AI provisions come into force on proclamation with regulations pending (enacted November 2024, not yet in force); the Treasury Board Directive has been in force since 2019 with a 2025-06-24 to 2026-06-24 compliance transition for the third-review updated requirements; OSFI Guideline E-23 is effective 2027-05-01.
- The held instruments' provisions (the Treasury Board Directive Section 6 requirement blocks and impact levels; the Algorithmic Impact Assessment 65/41 question structure; the ISED Code's six outcomes; the AI for All six pillars; the EDSTA proclamation clause; OSFI E-23; CAN/DGSI 101) were extracted verbatim from the held sources by a research subagent.
- Source discipline: AIDA and the EDSTA AI provisions are framed as NOT in force; OSFI E-23 as future-effective (2027); the Treasury Board Directive dates are the Directive's own, with 2025-06-24 correctly a compliance-transition date. The privacy layer is cross-referenced to the Canada privacy annex, not restated. All acronyms (AIDA, TBS, AIA, ISED, EDSTA, OSFI, FASTER, GBA Plus, CAN/DGSI) expanded on first use.
- All 67 gates pass on the working tree; PR-time checks green at the pre-push guard. Two independent adversarial verifiers (accuracy and completeness lenses) checked the annex given its flagship sensitivity.

### Discipline observation

The Canada annex is the flagship jurisdiction unit (most instruments, the AIDA-lapsed framing, the cross-annex privacy coordination) and was authored with the high-assurance treatment (upstream currency verification per fact, a source-not-held boundary, and dual independent adversarial verifiers). This PR carries the batched #799 `/validate-pr` record + history row + /retro row (recursion-avoidance) and corrects the PR-1 Treasury Board Directive date imprecision in-window.

### Added

## 2026-07-11, Library Version 2026.07.286, PR #798

AI gaps-and-expansion workstream PR 6c (board oversight, completing the split PR 6): creates [`ai/guide-ai-board-oversight.md`](../../ai/guide-ai-board-oversight.md) (A3), a director-facing guide for board-level oversight of the organization's AI use. It fills the confirmed gap that the AI domain had the executive AI Governance Council and AI Ethics Review Panel charters but nothing for the board's own oversight role. It is a Guide (guidance to the governing body), not a body-establishing charter, and is grounded in the trusted anchors ISO/IEC 38507:2022 (governing-body AI oversight, Clause 4.3 and Clause 6.2 to 6.7), ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Clause 5 leadership, and the NIST AI RMF GOVERN function; the recommendation-tier AICD/HTI Director's Guide is cited by reference only (CC BY-NC-SA, jurisdiction-specific), with no normative claim resting on it. Sections cover director duties and accountability, board AI literacy, setting AI risk appetite, a question set for directors, governance red flags, and board-reporting expectations (the receiving end of the AIGC and Ethics Panel escalation lines). A3 is listed in [`ai/README.md`](../../ai/README.md) and the [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md). ISO/IEC 38507:2022 and NIST AI RMF 1.0 upstream currency were confirmed this turn (both current; no revision published). Batches the #797 `/validate-pr` and `/retro` rows. Library `2026.07.285` to `2026.07.286`.

### Added

- [`ai/guide-ai-board-oversight.md`](../../ai/guide-ai-board-oversight.md) (`0.0.1`): new AI Board Oversight Guide (Owner Board Risk Committee, Approving Authority Board of Directors). Sections: purpose and scope; director duties and accountability (ISO/IEC 38507 Clause 4.3, NIST GOVERN 2.3); board AI literacy (NIST GOVERN 2.2); setting AI risk appetite and policy direction (38507 Clause 6.7, 42001 Clause 5.1 and 5.2); questions for directors (38507 Clause 6.2 to 6.7); governance red flags; board-reporting expectations (42001 Clause 9, NIST GOVERN 1.5); Framework alignment; Limitations.

### Changed

- [`ai/README.md`](../../ai/README.md) (`1.1.7`->`1.1.8`) and [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md) (`1.27.75`->`1.27.76`): A3 listing rows added.
- Generated artefacts ([`taxonomy.yml`](../../taxonomy.yml), [`docs/portal.md`](../../docs/portal.md), [`docs/maturity-scorecard.md`](../../docs/maturity-scorecard.md)) regenerated.

### Verification

- The Director's Guide trust status was confirmed: it is in the `publications/` bucket (untrusted by default) but SCREENED (2026-07-10, clean), CC BY-NC-SA. Resulting use constraint: cite-by-reference-only, no text embedded, no normative claim resting on it, and no import of its Australia-specific regulatory content into the jurisdiction-neutral corpus.
- The trusted backbone is ISO/IEC 38507:2022 (the standard written for the governing body, already cited in the AIGC charter), ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Clause 5, and NIST AI RMF GOVERN; each anchor was verified against the held text by the research subagent.
- Reference-currency SOP discharged this turn: ISO/IEC 38507:2022 confirmed current upstream (marked to-be-revised but no revision published) and NIST AI RMF (AI 100-1) confirmed at version 1.0 (no 2.0; next review by 2028), before the anchors became load-bearing.
- A3 wiring verified: orphan gate clean, listing-surface completeness clean, filename/title alignment clean, Owner/Approving roles (Board Risk Committee, Board of Directors) valid per the role register (gate 8); all 67 gates pass on the working tree.
- A3 is a Guide, not a Charter: it does not re-charter the AIGC or Ethics Panel; it cross-references them as the receiving end of their reporting and escalation lines.

### Discipline observation

PR 6c completes the split PR 6 (6a A4 maturity model #796, 6b governance/ethics expansions #797, 6c this A3 board-oversight guide). The listing surfaces (ai/README, doc-index register) were Version-bumped when A3's rows were added, applying the #796 /retro lesson. This PR carries the batched #797 `/validate-pr` record (clean), its history row, and the `/retro` row (recursion-avoidance).

## 2026-07-11, Library Version 2026.07.285, PR #797

AI gaps-and-expansion workstream PR 6b (governance and ethics expansions): expands four AI governance/ethics documents against ISO/IEC 42001, ISO/IEC 23894:2023, ISO/IEC 5338:2023, ISO/IEC 8183:2023, the OECD and UNESCO AI recommendations, CAN/DGSI 101:2025, and NIST (AI RMF, IR 8312, SP 1270). [`ai/framework-ai-governance-and-risk.md`](../../ai/framework-ai-governance-and-risk.md) gains Safety and Environmental/Sustainability as first-class control domains and a governance objective (both were absent), an Environmental classification criterion, and a new framework-alignment table anchoring its control domains to ISO/IEC 42001 Annex A, its objectives and risk classification to ISO/IEC 23894 Annex A/B, its lifecycle model to ISO/IEC 5338, and its data-primacy thesis to ISO/IEC 8183. [`ai/register-ai-risk.md`](../../ai/register-ai-risk.md) gains Safety and Environmental/Sustainability risk categories (both absent), the schema enumeration, two representative entries, and alignment anchors to ISO/IEC 23894 Annex A objectives, NIST AI RMF trustworthiness characteristics, NIST IR 8312, and NIST SP 1270. [`ai/guideline-ethical-ai-use.md`](../../ai/guideline-ethical-ai-use.md) gains a Sustainable and inclusive foundational principle (OECD Principle 1.1, the confirmed gap) and a UNESCO paragraph 26 anchor on its red lines. [`ai/charter-ai-ethics-review-panel.md`](../../ai/charter-ai-ethics-review-panel.md) anchors its hold-pending-reconsideration step to UNESCO paragraph 26, its record step to OECD Principle 1.5, and adds UNESCO and CAN/DGSI 101 alignment rows. Four research subagents verified every locator against the held texts, reconciling ISO/IEC 23894 (Annex A = 11 objectives including A.5 Environmental and A.10 Safety; Annex B = 7 risk sources) and correcting the plan (the register maps to Annex A objectives, not Annex B sources; OECD 1.5 is titled Accountability with traceability as its sub-point; the UNESCO red lines are the closing sentence of paragraph 26). Batches the #796 `/validate-pr` and `/retro` rows. Library `2026.07.284` to `2026.07.285`.

### Changed

- [`ai/framework-ai-governance-and-risk.md`](../../ai/framework-ai-governance-and-risk.md) (`1.1.3`->`1.1.4`): Safety and Environmental/Sustainability added as control domains and as governance objective 10; an Environmental classification criterion; a new Framework alignment table (42001 Annex A control groupings; 23894 Annex A objectives and Annex B risk sources; 5338 Clause 6 processes; 8183 data life cycle; NIST AI RMF; EU AI Act).
- [`ai/register-ai-risk.md`](../../ai/register-ai-risk.md) (`1.0.5`->`1.0.6`): Safety and Environmental/Sustainability risk categories; the Risk Category schema enumeration extended; two representative entries (AIR-2026-008 Safety, AIR-2026-009 Environmental); alignment rows for 23894 Annex A objectives, NIST AI RMF trustworthiness (Safe), NIST IR 8312, NIST SP 1270, ISO/IEC TR 24027.
- [`ai/guideline-ethical-ai-use.md`](../../ai/guideline-ethical-ai-use.md) (`1.0.3`->`1.0.4`): a Sustainable and inclusive foundational principle (OECD Principle 1.1); a UNESCO paragraph 26 anchor on the red lines; the OECD alignment row updated to name Principle 1.1.
- [`ai/charter-ai-ethics-review-panel.md`](../../ai/charter-ai-ethics-review-panel.md) (`1.0.1`->`1.0.2`): the hold-pending-reconsideration step anchored to UNESCO paragraph 26 (final human determination for irreversible decisions); the record step anchored to OECD Principle 1.5 (accountability and traceability); UNESCO and CAN/DGSI 101 alignment rows added.
- Generated artefacts ([`taxonomy.yml`](../../taxonomy.yml), [`docs/portal.md`](../../docs/portal.md), [`docs/maturity-scorecard.md`](../../docs/maturity-scorecard.md)) regenerated.

### Verification

- Four research subagents extracted the held ISO/IEC 23894:2023, ISO/IEC 5338:2023, ISO/IEC 8183:2023, ISO/IEC 42001 Annex A, OECD Recommendation, UNESCO Recommendation, CAN/DGSI 101:2025, NIST IR 8312, and NIST SP 1270 sources with verbatim locators.
- ISO/IEC 23894 reconciled against the held text: Annex A has 11 risk-management objectives (A.2 to A.12, including A.5 Environmental impact and A.10 Safety); Annex B has 7 risk sources (B.2 to B.8). The register's Risk Category maps to Annex A OBJECTIVES (an objective's negation is a category), NOT Annex B risk sources (the plan's 7-categories hypothesis was a coincidence; Annex B sources belong to the framework's risk classification and the register's Threat Source field).
- Both Safety and Environmental/Sustainability gaps were grep-confirmed ABSENT in the framework and the register before the additions.
- Plan corrections applied: OECD Principle 1.5 is titled Accountability (traceability is sub-point b), not Traceability; the UNESCO social-scoring and mass-surveillance red lines are the closing sentence of paragraph 26 (the same paragraph as the irreversibility rule).
- All 67 gates pass on the working tree; PR-time checks green at the pre-push guard.

### Discipline observation

PR 6b is the governance and ethics expansions arm of the split PR 6 (6a A4 created, 6c A3 to follow). This PR carries the batched #796 `/validate-pr` record (clean), its history row, and the `/retro` row (recursion-avoidance); the #796 /retro captures the listing-surface version-bump lesson from 6a.

## 2026-07-11, Library Version 2026.07.284, PR #796

AI gaps-and-expansion workstream PR 6a (maturity model): creates [`ai/framework-ai-maturity-model.md`](../../ai/framework-ai-maturity-model.md) (A4), the AI-domain maturity instrument that backs the AI Governance Council's previously-unbacked "AI maturity KPI". It is authored as the AI-domain deepening of the [`governance/standard-maturity-assessment-methodology.md`](../../governance/standard-maturity-assessment-methodology.md) (reusing that standard's five-tier CMMI ladder, its median-of-medians aggregation, and its floor-check, rather than defining a competing ladder), with eight assessment domains and three practices each adapted from the OWASP AI Maturity Assessment (AIMA) v1.0 (CC BY-SA 4.0, with attribution and share-alike) and the CMU SEI AI Adoption Maturity Model cited by reference. [`ai/charter-ai-governance-council.md`](../../ai/charter-ai-governance-council.md) is expanded to anchor its "AI maturity KPI" to the new framework and to strengthen its ISO/IEC 42001 (Section 5.1 to 5.3, A.3) and NIST AI RMF (GOVERN 2.3, 1.6) alignment anchors. A4 is listed in [`ai/README.md`](../../ai/README.md) and the [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md). Four research subagents extracted the held sources; the level-model choice (reuse the corpus's existing five-tier ladder rather than import AIMA's three-level or CMU SEI's five-level scale) is a stricter-safe, integrity-preserving default recorded in [`.working/pending-decisions.md`](../../.working/pending-decisions.md) for maintainer confirmation. Batches the #795 `/validate-pr` and `/retro` rows. Library `2026.07.283` to `2026.07.284`.

### Added

- [`ai/framework-ai-maturity-model.md`](../../ai/framework-ai-maturity-model.md) (`0.0.1`): new AI Maturity Model Framework. Sections: Purpose (backs the AIGC maturity KPI; the AI-domain deepening of the Maturity Assessment Methodology Standard), Scope (programme not document maturity), Governance, the reused five-tier ladder, eight assessment domains x three practices x two streams (Create and Promote / Measure and Improve), scoring and aggregation (median-of-medians + floor-check), the scorecard, Framework alignment, Limitations.

### Changed

- [`ai/charter-ai-governance-council.md`](../../ai/charter-ai-governance-council.md) (`1.2.5`->`1.2.6`): AI maturity KPI anchored to the new A4 framework; A4 added to Related Documents; the ISO/IEC 42001 Section 5 alignment row expanded to Section 5.1 to 5.3, an A.3 Internal organization row added, the NIST AI RMF row expanded to GOVERN 2.3 and GOVERN 1.6.
- [`ai/README.md`](../../ai/README.md) and [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md): A4 listing rows added.
- Generated artefacts ([`taxonomy.yml`](../../taxonomy.yml), [`docs/portal.md`](../../docs/portal.md), [`docs/maturity-scorecard.md`](../../docs/maturity-scorecard.md)) regenerated.

### Verification

- Four research subagents extracted the held OWASP AIMA v1.0 and CMU SEI AI Adoption Maturity Model structures; the OWASP AIMA CC BY-SA 4.0 license (adaptable) and the CMU SEI copyright restriction (cite-by-reference only) were confirmed, so A4 adapts the AIMA skeleton with attribution and cites CMU SEI by reference.
- Level-model decision recorded as a stricter-safe default: A4 reuses the corpus's existing five-tier CMMI ladder from the Maturity Assessment Methodology Standard rather than importing a third or fourth competing scale, keeping one maturity ladder across the corpus. Recorded in [`.working/pending-decisions.md`](../../.working/pending-decisions.md) for maintainer confirmation.
- A4 wiring verified: orphan gate clean (inbound reference from the AIGC charter), listing-surface completeness clean (register + domain README), filename/title alignment clean, metadata and language clean; all 67 gates pass on the working tree; PR-time checks green at the pre-push guard.
- A4 correctly does NOT feed the generated [`docs/maturity-scorecard.md`](../../docs/maturity-scorecard.md) (that is the document-maturity scorecard, a distinct surface); A4's hook is the AIGC maturity KPI.

### Discipline observation

PR 6 is split into 6a (this A4 creation), 6b (governance and ethics expansions), and 6c (the A3 board-oversight guide) per the plan's guidance and the always-split-when-in-doubt discipline. This PR carries the batched #795 `/validate-pr` record, its history row, and the `/retro` row (recursion-avoidance).

## 2026-07-11, Library Version 2026.07.283, PR #795

AI gaps-and-expansion workstream PR 5 (security, adversarial and agentic): anchors eight AI security documents against the flagship ETSI EN 304 223 V2.1.1 (2025-12) baseline cyber-security standard (uncited across the cluster until now), NIST SP 800-218A, the NIST AI 100-2e2025 adversarial-ML taxonomy, NIST AI 100-4, CSA AICM v1.1.0 / AI-CAIQ v1.1.0, and ISO/IEC 42006:2025; and closes the #794 `/validate-pr` citation-year finding. [`ai/standard-ai-security-and-risk.md`](../../ai/standard-ai-security-and-risk.md) gains a new framework-alignment table anchoring its minimum requirements to the ETSI principles (asset inventory 5.2.1, design-to-withstand-attacks 5.1.2 and threat management 5.1.3, human oversight 5.1.4, supply chain 5.2.3, secure end-of-life 5.5.1) and NIST AI 100-2e2025. [`ai/standard-ai-access-and-agent-permissions.md`](../../ai/standard-ai-access-and-agent-permissions.md) gains ETSI least-privilege provisions (5.1.2-6, 5.2.2-1, 5.2.2-3) and CSA AICM AIS-11 (Agents Security Boundaries) and AIS-13 (AI Sandboxing). [`ai/standard-ai-and-agentic-development-security.md`](../../ai/standard-ai-and-agentic-development-security.md) gains framework-anchor notes in its AI-assisted-development, supply-chain, and adversarial-testing sections (NIST SP 800-218A PW.4.4, PS.2.1, PS.3.2, PW.3.2, PW.3.3, PW.8; ETSI 5.2.2-3, 5.2.3-1, 5.2.4-1.2, 5.2.5) and AIS-13 in its §36 alignment table. [`ai/guide-ai-security-technical-implementation.md`](../../ai/guide-ai-security-technical-implementation.md) maps its A3 prompt-engineering patterns to AICM AIS-10/11/13/15 and adds NIST SP 800-218A and CSA AICM references. [`ai/guide-ai-adversarial-test-reference.md`](../../ai/guide-ai-adversarial-test-reference.md) and [`ai/guideline-adversarial-evaluation-suite-development.md`](../../ai/guideline-adversarial-evaluation-suite-development.md) gain NIST AI 100-2e2025, NIST SP 800-218A (PW.3.3, PW.8), and ETSI (5.1.3, 5.2.5) anchors (the guideline gains its first informative-references section). [`ai/template-ai-vendor-security-questionnaire.md`](../../ai/template-ai-vendor-security-questionnaire.md) gains four framework-alignment rows (CSA AICM/AI-CAIQ, ETSI supplier due diligence, NIST SP 800-218A shared-responsibility, NIST AI 100-4), a synthetic-content-provenance question, and an ISO/IEC 42006 accredited-certification-body question; [`ai/template-ai-red-team-report.md`](../../ai/template-ai-red-team-report.md) gains the NIST AI 100-2e2025 coverage axis. The #794 fix adds the edition year to the ISO/IEC 12792 and 25059 citations in [`ai/template-model-card.md`](../../ai/template-model-card.md) and [`ai/template-system-card.md`](../../ai/template-system-card.md). ETSI EN 304 223 upstream currency was confirmed this turn (held V2.1.1 (2025-12) equals the current published version). Four research subagents extracted the held sources and a refute-briefed skeptical verifier checked them, correcting several plan locators (ETSI least-privilege is 5.2.2-3 not 5.1.2; NIST SBOM/SLSA is PS.3.2 not PW.7.2). Batches the #794 `/validate-pr` and `/retro` rows. Library `2026.07.282` to `2026.07.283`.

### Changed

- [`ai/standard-ai-security-and-risk.md`](../../ai/standard-ai-security-and-risk.md) (`1.1.2`->`1.1.3`): new Section 7 framework-alignment table (five ETSI EN 304 223 provisions mapped to Sections 4.1/4.5/4.8/4.9/4.11, NIST AI 100-2e2025, and the standard high-level framework rows).
- [`ai/standard-ai-access-and-agent-permissions.md`](../../ai/standard-ai-access-and-agent-permissions.md) (`0.0.8`->`0.0.9`): three Section 11 rows: ETSI 5.1.2-6/5.2.2-1/5.2.2-3, CSA AICM AIS-11, AIS-13.

- [`ai/template-ai-vendor-security-questionnaire.md`](../../ai/template-ai-vendor-security-questionnaire.md) (`1.0.2`->`1.0.3`): four alignment rows (CSA AICM/AI-CAIQ v1.1.0, ETSI supplier due diligence, NIST SP 800-218A shared-responsibility, NIST AI 100-4); a synthetic-content-provenance question; an ISO/IEC 42006 accredited-body question.

### Verification

- ETSI EN 304 223 upstream currency confirmed this turn via the ETSI newsroom and the AI Standards Hub: the held V2.1.1 (2025-12) equals the current published version (it superseded the V2.0.0 draft). The reference-currency SOP (held-version-then-upstream) was satisfied before the flagship citations became load-bearing.
- Four research subagents extracted the held ETSI EN 304 223 V2.1.1, NIST SP 800-218A, NIST AI 100-2e2025, NIST AI 100-4, CSA AICM v1.1.0 / AI-CAIQ v1.1.0, and ISO/IEC 42006:2025 sources with verbatim clause quotes; a refute-briefed skeptical verifier checked the attributions.
- Plan-locator corrections applied from the research: ETSI dedicated-environment least-privilege is Provision 5.2.2-3 (not 5.1.2); NIST SBOM/SLSA is PS.3.2 (PW.7.2 is code review and model scanning); the ETSI 13-principles/5-phases structure and the provision-numbering form (cite specific provisions, not `-x` ranges) were confirmed against the held text.
- CSA AICM control codes (AIS-10, AIS-11, AIS-13, AIS-15, DSP-20, MDS-03, MDS-09) verified against the held AICM v1.1.0 catalogue and gate 48 (CCM/AICM citation-accuracy).
- Claim-fit guards: ISO/IEC 42006 cited for the credibility of an accredited certification (the vendor-facing requirement attributed to ISO/IEC 42001), not as a vendor obligation; NIST AI 100-4 cited as a taxonomy of provenance techniques, not a prescribed mechanism, with no specific scheme named.
- All 67 audit gates pass on the working tree; the PR-time checks (D1 to D7, plus history-aware 40/31) green at the pre-push guard.

### Discipline observation

This PR carries the batched #794 `/validate-pr` record, its history row, and the `/retro` row (recursion-avoidance), and closes the #794 in-window citation-year finding in the same diff as the PR-5 substantive work. The four research subagents corrected multiple plan-locator errors before they could ship, the research-assistant discipline working as designed on the workstream's most source-heavy cluster.

## 2026-07-11, Library Version 2026.07.282, PR #794

AI gaps-and-expansion workstream PR 4 (testing, evaluation, transparency, audit and certification): deepens seven AI documents against ISO/IEC 12792:2025, ISO/IEC TS 25058:2024, ISO/IEC 25059:2023, NIST IR 8312, ISO/IEC TR 24027:2021, and ISO/IEC 42006:2025, and closes the two in-window #793 `/validate-pr` findings. [`ai/framework-ai-model-documentation-and-transparency.md`](../../ai/framework-ai-model-documentation-and-transparency.md) Section 4 is restructured on the ISO/IEC 12792:2025 transparency taxonomy (four levels, Section 7 to Section 10; three objectives, Section 6.2; stakeholder pairs, Section 6.1 and Section 6.3), mapping each disclosure to its objective and pair. [`ai/standard-ai-testing-validation-and-documentation.md`](../../ai/standard-ai-testing-validation-and-documentation.md) Section 5 gains the ISO/IEC TS 25058:2024 Section 6.2 test methods, the ISO/IEC TR 24027:2021 Clause 7 fairness metrics, the previously-absent NIST IR 8312 explanation-accuracy and knowledge-limits principles, and the NIST AI 100-2e2025 attack taxonomy; [`ai/procedure-ai-evaluation.md`](../../ai/procedure-ai-evaluation.md) gains the same anchors (ISO/IEC TS 25058 was uncited though it is the on-point AI quality-evaluation guidance). [`ai/framework-ai-system-audit-certification.md`](../../ai/framework-ai-system-audit-certification.md) gains a Section 6.1 grounding the external certification body in ISO/IEC 42006:2025 (impartiality and no consulting, auditor competence, audit time, two-stage audit) and corrects the Section 10 alignment row that had mis-mapped 42006 to the internal-audit section; [`ai/procedure-ai-audit.md`](../../ai/procedure-ai-audit.md) gains a 42006 citation firewalled to the external third-party-certification path. The [`ai/template-model-card.md`](../../ai/template-model-card.md) and [`ai/template-system-card.md`](../../ai/template-system-card.md) templates gain content-tier fields (compute hardware and cost, environmental footprint; model-build procedure, hyperparameters, knowledge limits; bias-source categories; cross-jurisdiction data flow; user controllability and intervenability). The #793 fixes: a Constituent Models and Dependencies field added to [`ai/template-ai-system-register.md`](../../ai/template-ai-system-register.md) (resolving a foundation-procedure reference and a register integration-table claim to a field that did not exist), and a Step 10 clause in [`ai/procedure-ai-model-risk-assessment.md`](../../ai/procedure-ai-model-risk-assessment.md) confirming the provisional model-risk rating. Three research subagents extracted the held sources and a refute-briefed skeptical verifier checked them; the six sources' locators were verified against the held texts, with corrections applied (12792 Section 6.1 not Section 6.3 for the stakeholder-pair concept; Section 7.3.2 for environmental disclosures; Section 9.4.8 Computational costs and Section 9.4.7 compute hardware; 25059 Section 5.2 User controllability). Batches the #793 `/validate-pr` and `/retro` rows. Library `2026.07.281` to `2026.07.282`.

- [`ai/standard-ai-testing-validation-and-documentation.md`](../../ai/standard-ai-testing-validation-and-documentation.md) (`1.0.3`->`1.0.4`): Section 5.1 (TS 25058 Section 6.2 methods + 25059 Section 5.5 robustness), Section 5.2 (TR 24027 Clause 7 metrics), Section 5.3 (NIST IR 8312 Section 2.2 to Section 2.4 explanation accuracy and knowledge limits), Section 5.4 (NIST AI 100-2e2025 taxonomy); five alignment rows added; SQuaRE expanded on first use.

- Three research subagents extracted the held ISO/IEC 12792:2025, TS 25058:2024, 25059:2023, NIST IR 8312, TR 24027:2021, and ISO/IEC 42006:2025 sources with verbatim clause quotes; a refute-briefed skeptical verifier checked the attributions against the held texts.
- Plan-locator corrections applied from the research: 12792 stakeholder-pair concept is Section 6.1 (Section 6.3 is the enumerated list); the environmental-footprint material is Section 7.3.2 (no clause titled "environmental footprint"); Section 9.4.8 is "Computational costs" and Section 9.4.7 is the separate compute-hardware element; ISO/IEC 25059 Section 5.2 is "User controllability", definitionally distinct from plain controllability.
- Scope guards: 42006 kept on the AIMS-certification path (not EU per-system conformity), with an explicit firewall sentence; templates kept content-tier with no framework-alignment table (mirroring the template convention).
